summary:
Spinal subdural hematomas occur due to bridging vein rupture in the spine, not as a consequence of intracranial aneurysm rupture. Intracranial aneurysm rupture results in subarachnoid hemorrhage—a condition entirely distinct in location and mechanism—from spinal subdural hematoma.  

The two conditions affect non-overlapping anatomical sites and have independently derived pathological roots.

Intracranial aneurysm rupture results in subarachnoid hemorrhage—a condition entirely distinct in location and mechanism—from spinal subdural hematoma.  

The two conditions affect non-overlapping anatomical sites and have independently derived pathological roots.. Answer choice: B
